pr.title: Split out gmvsmelee (and 2 others) from start of gm_1A4C
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Split the first address range of the large nonmatching game-mode file gm_1A4C.c into three new source units: gmscdata.c for major/minor scene data accessors, gmmenu.c for main-menu-related OnLoad callbacks, and gmvsmelee.c for VS Melee major scene callbacks. The PR states gmvsmelee is a filename known from an assert string, while gmmenu and gmscdata are provisional names. Build configuration, splits, symbols, and headers were updated so the extracted ranges compile as separate NonMatching objects while preserving address layout.
what_changed: Moved gm_801A50A0 and gm_801A50AC from gm_1A4C.c into new gmscdata.c; these helper functions return the MinorSceneHandler array gm_803DA920 and MajorScene gm_803DACA4 respectively.
Created gmscdata.h and replaced direct extern declarations in gm_1A36.static.h and gm_1A4C.static.h with includes of the new public accessor header where needed.

Created gmmenu.c/gmmenu.h containing the unresolved placeholder declarations for gm_801A50B8_OnLoad, gm_801A5130_OnLoad, gm_801A51A8_OnLoad, and gm_801A5220_OnLoad. The PR body says this file covers scene ID 1 main menu OnLoad behavior but may still need further splitting.

Created gmvsmelee.c/gmvsmelee.h for functions starting at gm_801A5244 through gm_801A5F64, including implemented helpers gm_801A5244, gm_801A5250, and empty gm_801A5614_OnUnload, plus many unresolved function placeholders. The PR body says this filename is known from an assert string and handles Major Scene ID 2, VS Melee, with associated major scene callbacks.
Removed the extracted function declarations from gm_1A4C.h and the corresponding code/placeholders from the beginning of gm_1A4C.c, making gm_1A4C.c now start at gm_801A6254 in the split layout.

Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt to carve out gmscdata.c at text 0x801A50A0-0x801A50B8 and data 0x803DA920-0x803DB040, gmmenu.c at text 0x801A50B8-0x801A5244, and gmvsmelee.c at text 0x801A5244-0x801A6254, data 0x803DB040-0x803DB050, and sdata 0x804D4270-0x804D4278. gm_1A4C.c ranges were adjusted to begin after those extracted sections.
Updated configure.py to add gmscdata.c, gmmenu.c, and gmvsmelee.c as NonMatching objects before gm_1A4C.c.
Changed many *_MinorScenes symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from local to global scope, consistent with cross-file references after the split.
Added a block_relocations config.yml entry for target 0x80020000, documented as another bitmask rather than a pointer because it would point into the middle of a function.
Added an M2CTX-specific definition of NOT_IMPLEMENTED in placeholder.h, making it expand to nothing under M2CTX.
smart_moves: Used an assert-string-known filename, gmvsmelee, when available rather than inventing a name for the VS Melee file.
Clearly marked provisional names in the PR body: gmmenu and gmscdata are made-up names, with gmmenu explicitly flagged as potentially needing future splitting.

Separated data ownership/access into gmscdata.c: the large MajorScene and MinorSceneHandler arrays are described as being provided to other files solely through getter helpers, allowing the arrays to be localized behind a small public API.
Preserved build/link layout by updating splits.txt, symbols.txt scope, and configure.py together with the source split rather than only moving C code.
Kept unresolved functions as address-labeled placeholder declarations, retaining known boundaries while enabling file-level organization progress before full matching.
Used narrow new headers for extracted subsystems instead of leaving unrelated declarations in gm_1A4C.h/static headers.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large decomp file, update all three layers together: source/header declarations, configure.py object order, and splits.txt section ranges.
If extracted functions/data become referenced from multiple source files, symbol scope may need to change from local to global in symbols.txt.
Prefer a filename recovered from assert strings when available; otherwise document invented names and uncertainty so future work can rename or split without treating the label as canonical.
Large scene tables can often be hidden behind getter helpers. This reduces direct extern leakage and makes later static data ownership clearer.
For scene callback blobs, grouping by major scene ID or OnLoad role is a useful intermediate organization even if many functions remain nonmatching placeholders.
Documenting block relocation suppressions matters: the added 0x80020000 entry explains that the value is a bitmask, not a pointer, preventing future confusion around spurious relocations.
naming_conventions: Known filename from assert string: gmvsmelee for Major Scene ID 2 / VS Melee callbacks.
Provisional invented filename: gmmenu for main-menu-related OnLoad callbacks, with caveat that it contains other OnLoad callbacks and may need further split.
Provisional invented filename: gmscdata for major/minor scene data accessors and scene data arrays.
Header guards used MELEE_GM_VSMELEE_H, MELEE_GM_MENU_H, and MELEE_GM_SCDATA_H.
Address-based function names and suffixes were preserved, including _OnLoad, _OnInit, and _OnUnload callback suffixes such as gm_801A55EC_OnLoad and gm_801A5614_OnUnload.
Minor scene data symbols follow gm_<address>_MinorScenes naming, e.g. gm_803DD6A0_MinorScenes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was primarily a split/layout PR, not a function-matching PR; the new objects were added as NonMatching.
Maintained exact text/data/sdata boundaries in splits.txt to carve three new files out of the start of gm_1A4C without changing later gm_1A4C addresses.
Used placeholder markers like /// #gm_801A5258 for unresolved functions to preserve function boundaries in the new file.
Promoted MinorScenes symbols to global where cross-object references required visibility after splitting.
Added a block_relocations suppression for 0x80020000 after identifying it as a bitmask rather than a pointer-like relocation target.
Added an M2CTX NOT_IMPLEMENTED no-op define, likely to keep context generation or placeholder handling from emitting incompatible code under M2CTX.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for further splits or renames of gmmenu because this PR explicitly says it may need to be further split.
Search for later matching work in gmvsmelee.c functions gm_801A5258 through gm_801A5F64.
Investigate whether gmscdata.c later makes gm_803DA920 and gm_803DACA4 static as suggested by the PR body, since the diff excerpt still shows extern declarations in the new file.
Look for later PRs that rename address-based MinorScenes symbols to semantic scene-table names.
Check whether the M2CTX NOT_IMPLEMENTED change was required by this split or was a supporting context-generation fix reused elsewhere.
